alkylation reagent: alkylation using 15 mM iodoacetamide at room temperature for 30 min in the dark.
alkylation concentration: alkylation using 15 mM iodoacetamide at room temperature for 30 min in the dark.
cleavage agent: For enzymatic digestion, trypsin (20 µg; Promega Corporation, Madison, WI, USA) was added at a 1:50 (w/w) enzyme-to-substrate ratio.
collision energy: with a normalized collision energy of 35%.
enrichment method: using affinity enrichment with anti-Kla antibodies
fragmentation method: Fragmentation was carried out using collision-induced dissociation (CID) with a normalized collision energy of 35%.
instrument: we employed immunoprecipitation (IP) using pan-anti-lysine lactylation antibodies and an LTQ-Orbitrap Velos mass spectrometer.
ionization type: The electrospray ionization source was operated at a voltage of 1.8 kV.
reduction reagent: reduced with 5 mM dithiothreitol at 55˚C for 30 min.
reduction concentration: reduced with 5 mM dithiothreitol at 55˚C for 30 min.
